title: Emit Kubernetes Events for key lifecycle transitions
description:
# GitHub Issue #185: Emit Kubernetes Events for key lifecycle transitions using the existing Recorder

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: The KeystoneReconciler has an unused Recorder field (keystone_controller.go:52). Wire it into all sub-reconcilers to emit Events for significant lifecycle transitions: bootstrap completion, database migration phases (expand/migrate/contract), fernet/credential key rotation, upgrade start/completion, and error conditions. Use Normal type for success and Warning type for failures.

**Motivation**: Operators relying on kubectl describe keystone or event-based alerting currently get zero signal about what the controller is doing. Events are the standard Kubernetes mechanism for surfacing controller activity and are critical for operational visibility, especially during upgrades and key rotations.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Events emitted for: bootstrap success/failure, each upgrade phase transition, key rotation success/failure, deployment rollout
- [ ] Events visible via kubectl describe keystone and kubectl get events
- [ ] Event reasons are structured and documented for use in alerting rules
- [ ] Unit tests verify event emission for each lifecycle transition

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/185

stories.title: Cluster operator monitors bootstrap lifecycle via kubectl describe
stories.want: to see Kubernetes events when Keystone bootstrap succeeds or fails
stories.so_that: I can monitor initial deployment progress via kubectl describe keystone without reading controller logs
criteria: Normal event with reason BootstrapComplete emitted when bootstrap Job succeeds
Warning event with reason BootstrapFailed emitted when bootstrap Job fails
Events are visible via kubectl describe keystone in the Events section
Event messages include enough context to understand the transition

stories.title: Cluster operator tracks upgrade progress via events
stories.want: to see events for each upgrade phase transition (expand, migrate, rolling update, contract, completion)
stories.so_that: I can track rolling upgrade progress in real-time without reading controller logs
criteria: Normal event emitted when upgrade is initiated with from/to release versions
Normal events emitted when expand, migrate, and contract phases complete
Normal event emitted when entire upgrade completes
Warning events emitted when any upgrade phase job fails
Warning events emitted for validation errors (version parse, downgrade, non-sequential)

stories.title: Cluster operator monitors database sync lifecycle
stories.want: to see events when database schema sync succeeds, fails, or detects drift
stories.so_that: I can identify database-related issues quickly during non-upgrade reconciliation
criteria: Normal event emitted when db_sync and schema-check complete successfully
Warning event emitted when db_sync job fails
Warning event emitted when schema drift is detected after db_sync

stories.title: Cluster operator confirms key generation via events
stories.want: to see events when fernet and credential encryption keys are initially generated
stories.so_that: I can confirm the key management lifecycle initialized correctly
criteria: Normal event with reason FernetKeysGenerated emitted when initial fernet keys Secret is created
Normal event with reason CredentialKeysGenerated emitted when initial credential keys Secret is created
Events are only emitted on initial creation, not on every reconcile loop

stories.title: SRE configures alerting rules from structured event reasons
stories.want: structured, stable event reason strings documented in reference docs
stories.so_that: I can configure event-based alerting rules (e.g., Prometheus Alertmanager, kubectl get events --field-selector) that remain stable across controller versions
criteria: All event reasons use PascalCase identifiers (e.g., BootstrapFailed, UpgradeComplete)
Reference documentation lists all event reasons with their type (Normal/Warning) and when they fire
Warning type is used consistently for failures and error conditions
Normal type is used consistently for successful transitions

stories.title: Developer verifies event emission with unit tests
stories.want: unit tests that assert correct event emission for each lifecycle transition
stories.so_that: future code changes cannot accidentally drop events without test failures
criteria: Each event-emitting code path has a corresponding test assertion using FakeRecorder
Tests verify both the event type (Normal/Warning) and reason string
Tests are added to existing test functions that already exercise the relevant code paths
FakeRecorder Events channel is asserted with Receive matcher from gomega

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL emit Kubernetes events for bootstrap lifecycle transitions
requirements.rationale: Bootstrap success/failure is a critical lifecycle event that operators need visibility into without reading controller logs

scenarios.name: Bootstrap job completes successfully
scenarios.when: reconcileBootstrap detects the bootstrap Job has succeeded
scenarios.then: a Normal event with reason BootstrapComplete is emitted on the Keystone CR
and_then: the BootstrapReady condition is also set to True

scenarios.name: Bootstrap job fails permanently
scenarios.when: reconcileBootstrap detects the bootstrap Job has failed
scenarios.then: a Warning event with reason BootstrapFailed is emitted on the Keystone CR
and_then: the event message includes the error details

scenarios.name: Bootstrap job still running
scenarios.when: reconcileBootstrap detects the bootstrap Job is still in progress
scenarios.then: no event is emitted (to avoid noisy repeated events on each requeue)

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL emit Kubernetes events for non-upgrade database sync lifecycle transitions
requirements.rationale: Database schema sync success and failure are critical operational signals for diagnosing deployment issues

scenarios.name: db_sync and schema-check complete successfully
scenarios.when: reconcileDatabase completes db_sync and schema-check jobs without errors in non-upgrade mode
scenarios.then: a Normal event with reason DatabaseSynced is emitted on the Keystone CR
and_then: the DatabaseReady condition is set to True

scenarios.name: db_sync job fails
scenarios.when: the db_sync Job fails during non-upgrade reconciliation
scenarios.then: a Warning event with reason DBSyncFailed is emitted on the Keystone CR
and_then: the event message includes the error details

scenarios.name: Schema drift detected after db_sync
scenarios.when: the schema-check Job fails after a successful db_sync
scenarios.then: a Warning event with reason SchemaDriftDetected is emitted on the Keystone CR
and_then: the DatabaseReady condition is set to False

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL emit Kubernetes events for all upgrade phase transitions and validation errors
requirements.rationale: Upgrade progress is the most critical operational signal; operators need to track each phase without controller log access

scenarios.name: Upgrade initiated
scenarios.when: initiateUpgrade validates the upgrade path and sets the initial expand phase
scenarios.then: a Normal event with reason UpgradeInitiated is emitted with from/to release versions
and_then: the UpgradePhase status is set to Expanding

scenarios.name: Expand phase completes
scenarios.when: the expand db_sync Job completes successfully
scenarios.then: a Normal event with reason ExpandComplete is emitted

scenarios.name: Migrate phase completes
scenarios.when: the migrate db_sync Job completes successfully
scenarios.then: a Normal event with reason MigrateComplete is emitted

scenarios.name: Contract phase completes (upgrade finished)
scenarios.when: the contract db_sync Job completes successfully
scenarios.then: a Normal event with reason UpgradeComplete is emitted with from/to release versions
and_then: the UpgradePhase and TargetRelease are cleared

scenarios.name: Upgrade phase job fails
scenarios.when: any upgrade phase job (expand, migrate, or contract) fails
scenarios.then: a Warning event with reason {Phase}Failed (e.g., ExpandFailed) is emitted
and_then: the event message includes the failed job name and error

scenarios.name: Version parse error blocks upgrade
scenarios.when: ParseRelease fails for either installed or target release tag
scenarios.then: a Warning event with reason VersionParseError is emitted

scenarios.name: Downgrade not supported
scenarios.when: the target release is older than the installed release
scenarios.then: a Warning event with reason DowngradeNotSupported is emitted

scenarios.name: Non-sequential upgrade path
scenarios.when: the upgrade path skips a release version
scenarios.then: a Warning event with reason UpgradePathInvalid is emitted

scenarios.name: Image tag changed during active upgrade
scenarios.when: spec.image.tag differs from status.targetRelease during an active upgrade
scenarios.then: a Warning event with reason UpgradeTargetChanged is emitted
and_then: the reconciler returns an error to prevent the conflicting change

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL emit Kubernetes events when fernet or credential encryption keys are initially generated
requirements.rationale: Key generation is a one-time lifecycle event that confirms the encryption key management subsystem initialized correctly

scenarios.name: Initial fernet keys generated
scenarios.when: reconcileFernetKeys creates the fernet keys Secret for the first time
scenarios.then: a Normal event with reason FernetKeysGenerated is emitted on the Keystone CR
and_then: the FernetKeysReady condition is set to False/GeneratingKeys

scenarios.name: Fernet keys Secret already exists
scenarios.when: reconcileFernetKeys finds the fernet keys Secret already exists
scenarios.then: no FernetKeysGenerated event is emitted (to avoid noisy repeated events)

scenarios.name: Initial credential keys generated
scenarios.when: reconcileCredentialKeys creates the credential keys Secret for the first time
scenarios.then: a Normal event with reason CredentialKeysGenerated is emitted on the Keystone CR
and_then: the CredentialKeysReady condition is set to False/GeneratingKeys

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL emit a Kubernetes event when the Deployment rollout completes during an upgrade
requirements.rationale: Deployment rollout completion during an upgrade is a critical transition that triggers the contract phase

scenarios.name: Deployment rollout completes during upgrade
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment detects the Deployment is ready while UpgradePhase is RollingUpdate
scenarios.then: a Normal event with reason DeploymentRolloutComplete is emitted

scenarios.name: Deployment ready in steady state
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment detects the Deployment is ready outside of an upgrade
scenarios.then: no deployment rollout event is emitted (steady-state, not a transition)

requirements.description: Events SHALL use Normal type for successful transitions and Warning type for failures
requirements.rationale: Consistent event type usage enables kubectl get events --field-selector type=Warning filtering for operational triage

scenarios.name: Success transition uses Normal type
scenarios.when: any lifecycle transition completes successfully (bootstrap, db_sync, upgrade phase, key generation)
scenarios.then: the event is emitted with corev1.EventTypeNormal

scenarios.name: Failure condition uses Warning type
scenarios.when: any lifecycle transition fails or a validation error occurs
scenarios.then: the event is emitted with corev1.EventTypeWarning

scenarios.name: FakeRecorder format encodes event type
scenarios.when: a unit test checks the FakeRecorder Events channel
scenarios.then: the event string starts with 'Normal' or 'Warning' followed by the reason
and_then: this format allows tests to assert both type and reason in one check

requirements.description: Event reasons SHALL be stable PascalCase identifiers suitable for alerting rules
requirements.rationale: Alerting rules depend on stable reason strings; changes would silently break monitoring

scenarios.name: Event reason is PascalCase without spaces
scenarios.when: any event is emitted
scenarios.then: the reason string is a PascalCase identifier (e.g., BootstrapComplete, DBSyncFailed)

scenarios.name: Event reasons documented in reference doc
scenarios.when: an SRE looks up available event reasons for alerting configuration
scenarios.then: the reference documentation lists all event reasons with type, trigger condition, and example message

requirements.description: Unit tests SHALL verify event emission for each lifecycle transition using FakeRecorder
requirements.rationale: Without test coverage, events could be silently dropped during refactoring

scenarios.name: Event assertion added to existing test
scenarios.when: a test exercises a code path that emits an event
scenarios.then: the test asserts the FakeRecorder Events channel received the expected event string
and_then: the assertion checks both event type and reason via ContainSubstring

scenarios.name: No spurious events in negative cases
scenarios.when: a test exercises a code path that should NOT emit an event (e.g., in-progress polling)
scenarios.then: the FakeRecorder Events channel is empty after the reconcile call

tasks.title: Add Kubernetes events to reconcileBootstrap and verify with unit test assertions (REQ-001, REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go, add r.Recorder.Eventf() calls: (1) Warning/BootstrapFailed after the condition at line 32-39, (2) Normal/BootstrapComplete after the condition at line 53-59. In reconcile_bootstrap_test.go, add event assertions to TestReconcileBootstrap_JobComplete (assert Normal BootstrapComplete), TestReconcileBootstrap_JobFailed (assert Warning BootstrapFailed), and TestReconcileBootstrap_JobRunning (assert no event emitted). Use r.Recorder.(*record.FakeRecorder).Events with gomega Receive matcher.

tasks.title: Add Kubernetes event to reconcileFernetKeys for initial key generation and verify with unit test assertions (REQ-004, REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_fernet.go, add r.Recorder.Event() call: Normal/FernetKeysGenerated after the createFernetKeysSecret call succeeds at line 72, before setting the GeneratingKeys condition. In reconcile_fernet_test.go, add event assertion to TestReconcileFernetKeys_NoSecret_CreatesSecretAndRequeues (assert Normal FernetKeysGenerated), and verify TestReconcileFernetKeys_SecretExists does NOT emit FernetKeysGenerated.

tasks.title: Add Kubernetes event to reconcileCredentialKeys for initial key generation and verify with unit test assertions (REQ-004, REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_credential.go, add r.Recorder.Event() call: Normal/CredentialKeysGenerated after the createCredentialKeysSecret call succeeds at line 80, before setting the GeneratingKeys condition. In reconcile_credential_test.go, add event assertion to TestReconcileCredentialKeys_NoSecret_CreatesSecretAndRequeues (assert Normal CredentialKeysGenerated), and verify TestReconcileCredentialKeys_SecretExists does NOT emit CredentialKeysGenerated.

tasks.title: Add Kubernetes events to reconcileDatabase non-upgrade path and verify with unit test assertions (REQ-002, REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go, add r.Recorder.Eventf() calls for the non-upgrade path: (1) Warning/DBSyncFailed after the condition at line 163-169, (2) Warning/SchemaDriftDetected after the condition at line 187-193, (3) Normal/DatabaseSynced after the condition at line 212-218. In reconcile_database_test.go, add event assertions to existing tests that exercise the brownfield db_sync path: assert DBSyncFailed on job failure, SchemaDriftDetected on schema-check failure, and DatabaseSynced on success.

tasks.title: Add Kubernetes events to upgrade initiation, validation, and phase transitions in reconcileDatabase and verify with unit test assertions (REQ-003, REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go, add r.Recorder.Eventf() calls for: (1) Warning/UpgradeTargetChanged at line 141-151, (2) Warning/VersionParseError at lines 253-260 and 265-272, (3) Warning/DowngradeNotSupported at line 276-283, (4) Warning/UpgradePathInvalid at line 287-294, (5) Normal/UpgradeInitiated at line 299-308, (6) Warning/ExpandFailed after setUpgradeJobFailed call in reconcileExpand at line 364, (7) Normal/ExpandComplete at line 373-381, (8) Warning/MigrateFailed in reconcileMigrate at line 393, (9) Normal/MigrateComplete at line 401-410, (10) Warning/ContractFailed in reconcileContract at line 431, (11) Normal/UpgradeComplete at line 440-454. In reconcile_database_test.go, add event assertions to the existing upgrade tests covering each scenario.

tasks.title: Add Kubernetes event to reconcileDeployment for upgrade rollout completion and verify with unit test assertions (REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go, add r.Recorder.Eventf() call: Normal/DeploymentRolloutComplete when the Deployment becomes ready during UpgradePhaseRollingUpdate at lines 104-116, before transitioning to Contracting. In reconcile_deployment_test.go, add event assertion to the existing test that covers the RollingUpdate->Contracting transition (assert Normal DeploymentRolloutComplete), and verify the normal deployment-ready path does NOT emit a rollout event.

tasks.title: Create reference documentation for Keystone controller event reasons (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Create docs/reference/keystone-events.md documenting all event reasons emitted by the Keystone controller (CC-0070). Include a table with columns: Reason, Type (Normal/Warning), Sub-reconciler, Trigger Condition, Example Message. Cover all 20 events across bootstrap (2), database non-upgrade (3), database upgrade (11), fernet (1), credential (1), deployment (1). Include a section on using event reasons for alerting rule configuration with kubectl get events examples. Add cross-reference to the existing keystone-reconciler.md doc.

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator monitors bootstrap lifecycle via kubectl describe

test_specifications.expected: FakeRecorder Events channel should receive string containing 'Warning ContractFailed' when contract Job fails

review_criteria: All lifecycle transitions listed in the acceptance criteria emit Kubernetes events: bootstrap success/failure, upgrade phase transitions, key rotation success, deployment rollout, db_sync success/failure
Normal event type used for all success/completion events; Warning type used for all failure/error events — no exceptions
Event reason strings are stable PascalCase identifiers matching the documented set in the reference doc
Unit tests for every event-emitting code path use FakeRecorder with Receive/ContainSubstring assertions
No events emitted for polling/in-progress states (BootstrapInProgress, DBSyncInProgress, etc.) to avoid noisy repeated events
Events are emitted alongside existing condition-setting code, not replacing it — conditions and events serve complementary purposes
Reference documentation at docs/reference/keystone-events.md lists all event reasons with type, trigger condition, and example message
go vet and golangci-lint pass without errors on all modified files
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

The KeystoneReconciler already has a `Recorder record.EventRecorder` field (keystone_controller.go:52), initialized in main.go:47 via `mgr.GetEventRecorderFor("keystone-controller")`. RBAC for event creation is already declared (keystone_controller.go:60). All sub-reconcilers are methods on `*KeystoneReconciler` and access the recorder via `r.Recorder`.

## Event Emission Pattern

Use `r.Recorder.Event(keystone, corev1.EventTypeNormal, "Reason", "message")` for simple messages and `r.Recorder.Eventf(keystone, corev1.EventTypeWarning, "Reason", "format %s", args...)` for messages with dynamic content. Place event emission calls immediately after the corresponding `conditions.SetCondition()` call.

## Test Pattern

All existing tests already create `record.NewFakeRecorder(10)`. To assert events:
```go
fakeRec := r.Recorder.(*record.FakeRecorder)
g.Expect(fakeRec.Events).To(Receive(ContainSubstring("Normal BootstrapComplete")))
```
For negative assertions (no event expected):
```go
g.Expect(fakeRec.Events).To(BeEmpty())
// or
g.Expect(fakeRec.Events).ToNot(Receive())
```

## Event Flow Diagram

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ KeystoneReconciler.Reconcile()                   │
│                                                  │
│  reconcileBootstrap()                            │
│   ├─ Success → Event(Normal, BootstrapComplete)  │
│   └─ Failure → Event(Warning, BootstrapFailed)   │
│                                                  │
│  reconcileDatabase()                             │
│   ├─ DB Sync OK → Event(Normal, DatabaseSynced)  │
│   ├─ DB Sync Fail → Event(Warning, DBSyncFailed) │
│   ├─ Schema Drift → Event(Warning, SchemaDrift)  │
│   └─ Upgrade Flow:                               │
│       ├─ Init → Event(Normal, UpgradeInitiated)  │
│       ├─ Expand OK → Event(Normal, ExpandComplete)│
│       ├─ Migrate OK → Event(Normal, MigrateComp) │
│       ├─ Contract OK → Event(Normal, UpgradComp) │
│       └─ Any Fail → Event(Warning, {Phase}Failed)│
│                                                  │
│  reconcileFernetKeys()                           │
│   └─ New Secret → Event(Normal, FernetKeysGen)   │
│                                                  │
│  reconcileCredentialKeys()                       │
│   └─ New Secret → Event(Normal, CredKeysGen)     │
│                                                  │
│  reconcileDeployment()                           │
│   └─ Upgrade Rollout → Event(Normal, Rollout)    │
│                                                  │
│  Events → K8s API → etcd                         │
│  Visible: kubectl describe keystone              │
│           kubectl get events                     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Pitfalls

1. **Do NOT emit events in polling/in-progress states** (e.g., BootstrapInProgress, DBSyncInProgress) — these fire on every requeue and create noise without actionable information.
2. **Event deduplication**: K8s API server deduplicates events by (involvedObject, reason, message, source). Same event emitted multiple times just increments a counter. This makes it safe to emit on condition-setting code paths that may run multiple times.
3. **FakeRecorder buffer**: Tests use buffer size 10. If a reconcile path emits more than 10 events, the channel blocks. The maximum events per reconcile call in this design is 2 (e.g., one success + one transition), well within the buffer.
4. **corev1 import**: All affected files already import corev1 for other purposes (container specs, secrets, etc.), so no new imports are needed except in reconcile_bootstrap.go which needs corev1 added.
